title: chamaecrista nictitans subsp. patellaria
intro: Chamaecrista nictitans subsp. patellaria, also known as sensitive plant, is a perennial herb that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, and it is known for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chamaecrista nictitans subsp. patellaria include its ability to help manage diabetes, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of malaria. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
